key_insight: Geometric correction terms can restore the Raviart–Thomas div-conformity lost on distorted quadrilateral meshes while preserving the original element size and degrees of freedom.
engineering_relevance: This improves mixed finite element flux simulations on practical non-ideal meshes, giving more reliable accuracy for elliptic problems in engineering workflows.
method: Modify local Raviart–Thomas basis functions with geometry-aware correction terms.
Compensate for bilinear-mapping distortion on quadrilateral elements.
Apply the framework to lowest- and next-to-lowest-order RT elements without changing DOFs.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports optimal approximation theory plus numerical experiments on distorted meshes showing the modified elements converge as predicted and outperform classical RT as distortion grows.
watch_item: The benefit depends on quadrilateral mesh shape-regularity and on implementing the correction terms consistently with the geometric mapping.
paper_title: Raviart--Thomas Elements with Geometric Correction for Distorted Quadrilateral Meshes
